title: The Hacker's Toolkit
description: A practical tour through ciphers that teach core cryptanalysis techniques — frequency analysis, pattern recognition, and algorithmic thinking.

stops.label: Caesar Cipher
stops.note: Start simple: brute-force 25 keys, then learn frequency analysis.

stops.label: Monoalphabetic Sub.
stops.note: 26! possible keys — but frequency analysis makes it solvable.

stops.label: Homophonic Sub.
stops.note: Multiple symbols per letter flatten frequencies — defeating simple analysis.

stops.label: Vigenère Cipher
stops.note: Kasiski examination and IC analysis — finding the key length is the breakthrough.

stops.label: Playfair
stops.note: Digraph substitution — frequency analysis on letter pairs instead of singles.

stops.label: Columnar Transposition
stops.note: Anagramming columns — using frequency and contact analysis.

stops.label: Double Transposition
stops.note: Two passes multiply complexity — a favorite of SOE in WWII.

stops.label: Enigma
stops.note: Cribs, known-plaintext, and the Bombe — industrial-scale cryptanalysis.

stops.label: Codebreaker's Workbench
stops.note: Put your skills to work — analyze real ciphertext with live tools.
